description:
You are a Level-2 support engineer who must collect a quick health-check report from a local SQLite database that backs an internal shop application.

    Current environment layout that you will find when the terminal session starts:
        • /home/user/support/db/           – already exists   
        • /home/user/support/db/shop.db    – the live SQLite database you must interrogate  
        • /home/user/support/diag/         – does NOT exist yet (you must create it)  

    Goal  
        1. Interrogate /home/user/support/db/shop.db for basic statistics.  
        2. Run an integrity check on the database.  
        3. Store everything in a single diagnostic text file at  
           /home/user/support/diag/healthcheck.log

    Required SQL queries  
        • Total number of rows in table customers.  
        • Total number of rows in table orders.  
        • Number of rows in table orders for each status value OPEN, SHIPPED, CANCELLED.  
        • PRAGMA integrity_check; (the result should normally be “ok”).  

    VERY STRICT log-file format  
        The healthcheck.log file **must** contain exactly the eight lines shown below, with the same wording, colon placement, indentation and spacing.  
        The test harness is case-sensitive and will treat any deviation as failure.  
        Substitute the angle-bracket placeholders with the actual data you retrieve:  

        ==== SHOP DB DIAGNOSTICS ====  
        Timestamp: <ISO-8601 date-time in UTC, e.g. 2023-07-11T14:23:08Z>  
        Total_Customers: <integer>  
        Total_Orders: <integer>  
        Orders_By_Status:  
          OPEN: <integer>  
          SHIPPED: <integer>  
          CANCELLED: <integer>  
        DBIntegrity: <string returned by PRAGMA integrity_check>  
        ==== END ====  

        Notes on the format:  
          • “Orders_By_Status:” is followed by exactly three lines, each starting with two spaces.  
          • The block is book-ended by the first and last “==== … ====” lines.  
          • Use a single space after every colon.  
          • No extra blank lines anywhere in the file.  

    Sequence overview (for your benefit; do NOT echo this in the log file):  
        a. Create /home/user/support/diag/ if it is missing.  
        b. Use the sqlite3 CLI (or any other shell method) to collect the required metrics.  
        c. Build the healthcheck.log file in the exact format above.  
        d. Inspect the file with cat so the user (grader) can see it in the terminal output.  

    Verification  
        Automated tests will:  
          • Confirm that /home/user/support/diag/healthcheck.log exists.  
          • Parse its eight mandatory lines literally (except the Timestamp, whose value is ignored as long as it is valid ISO-8601).  
          • Match the numerical values you recorded with the true database contents.  
          • Ensure the integrity result is “ok”.  

    Reminder  
        • You do NOT have sudo rights. Stay inside /home/user.  
        • Only the final healthcheck.log file matters; intermediate scratch work will be ignored.  
        • If you rerun the command sequence, make sure you overwrite the old log so the file always contains one—and only one—diagnostic block.
truth:
Initial database state (already present before the task begins)
    --------------------------------------------------------------
    Location: /home/user/support/db/shop.db
    Schema:
        CREATE TABLE customers (
            id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            name       TEXT NOT NULL,
            email      TEXT NOT NULL,
            created_at TEXT NOT NULL
        );

        CREATE TABLE orders (
            id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            customer_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
            amount     REAL NOT NULL,
            status     TEXT NOT NULL,
            created_at TEXT NOT NULL
        );

    Seed data:
        customers
            (1, "Alice Smith",   "alice@example.com",   "2023-01-03 11:34:21")
            (2, "Bob Johnson",   "bob@example.com",     "2023-02-18 09:12:55")
            (3, "Charlie Brown", "charlie@example.com", "2023-03-22 17:49:07")

        orders
            (1, 1, 120.50, "OPEN",      "2023-04-01 12:00:00")
            (2, 2,  89.99, "SHIPPED",   "2023-04-02 14:00:00")
            (3, 3,  45.00, "SHIPPED",   "2023-04-03 16:30:00")
            (4, 1,  67.25, "CANCELLED", "2023-04-04 18:45:00")
            (5, 2, 199.95, "OPEN",      "2023-04-05 10:15:00")

    Ground-truth counts expected by the grader
        Total_Customers = 3
        Total_Orders    = 5
        OPEN            = 2
        SHIPPED         = 2
        CANCELLED       = 1
        PRAGMA integrity_check output = "ok"

    The grader will open /home/user/support/diag/healthcheck.log and expect the following literal content (except the timestamp, which is ignored):

        ==== SHOP DB DIAGNOSTICS ====
        Timestamp: *ignored during grading*
        Total_Customers: 3
        Total_Orders: 5
        Orders_By_Status:
          OPEN: 2
          SHIPPED: 2
          CANCELLED: 1
        DBIntegrity: ok
        ==== END ====
